reviews.review_text:
I LOVED this game, and would come back to it every couple months to sink a week or so in.

But man it's gone downhill. Updates feel shoddy and AI made, things constantly not working. The Delivery system feels so bad in the game, the DLCs are minor updates that don't really work and cost money. 

Really sad to see how bad this game has gotten. A genuine "was better before full release" tragic tale.

This game is done now we have to pay for bakery and other stuff like dlc which you can get for free in othrs games they have multiple goods funiture im notgonna pay 4 or 5 dollars for each dlc i can just pay enough for other simulator and can find more option then here i preffere cafe simlators and others simulator but not this one im done with this game and with this overpriced dlc. what next now they will put dlc of funiture, and other each types of goods. im realy done with this game

the game is pretty fun up until about 10-15 hours in, the progression just gets impossibly grindy and slow. Feels like it takes 20 in game days to get one new license or one new expansion to the store, I just never feel like I am progressing after a certain point.

releasing 6 different DLCs with a total 15-30 dollar price tag (depends on the region) is so stupid. If they were worth it yea sure but it feels like they are just simple updates that would have made the playerbase happier. 

Now I want to play the game but I don't want to pay 15 to 30 dollars just for basic updates adding different types of products and it kinda feels like half a game I guess...

One of the first of the "retail simulator" games that exploded on Steam a few years ago. Developer is attentive to the community and releases frequent updates to keep the game as bug-free and fresh as possible. However there have been several DLC packs released with a lot of negative reviews. Looking them over, it looks like a bit of a cash grab. Products that don't fit on your shelves, items that don't scan, etc. 

Overall the game is fun and even includes multiplayer (I bought a copy for my entire family). It's a solid retail simulator and hits all of the retail simulator marks. Customer satisfaction, product management, store maintenance, etc. Updates beyond 1.0 saw new things like vehicles added, picking up products from local vendors, and expanding the base gameplay. 

There are still bugs and the DLC should just be part of the core game, but at the end of the day if you want to sit back and build a supermarket empire, you won't be disappointed with this.
Great game! Just needs some smoother, more dynamic animation. I'd love to see sims2 level interaction animations with the cashiers and more!

This used to be an awesome game to play! And as newer builds were coming out I was extremely excited, however, my love for both the game and the development team completely dissipated when they added their banner here on the store page. If you are anyone who knows AI, you will easily be able to tell that there is a high likely chance (almost certainty for me) that the image was created using AI. The developers backing this "anonymous" artist shows that they care not for their community and those that would have genuinely loved to have made REAL art for them. Instead they decided to choose a harmful and lazy option. PLUS unlike other companies/games that list the use of AI in the product, this one does not have this label AT ALL! (And if it does and I am blind, apologies) WHICH the general use of AI is my main reason to remove the game from my steam library. 

Another reason is that previous optimizations were just not being kept up with the addition of new features and correction of bugs. I do understand that the game technically still WAS in early access when I played, so this point of contention is probably mute. However, I refuse to play it once more to see if any thing has improved and so I apologize to anyone, who doesn't let the common use of AI to deter them from their game, that was looking for more helpful and up to date specs/information on the game.
There are better priced and better market simulators. greedy devs instead of producing a complete game made their expansions DLCs.

It's good that it's cross platform for multiplayer, but there has been issues with saving lately. After about 5 mins of playing the menu cannot be accessed, and the quicksave option also dies out. Had to restart the day multiple times. Please fix this issue asap, as of now it's unplayable.

I loved this game until i got way farther, I had spent over 40 hours growing my supermarket. But once you get the higher loan options you get caught in a cycle where you cant stock your shelves because you cant make the daily loan payments so they snowball and get bigger and bigger so you never have any money to buy more product to sell so you cant make more money. Taking out the other loans to try to help pay it off just makes it worse, no matter what i did i could not get out of debt. 40 hours in the game completely lost because i went bankrupt. I did not even know the game had that?? Would have been nice to know before i lost absolutely everything. Once its gone their is no way to go back to a certain point in the save and try a different route, nothing. How do you expect people to continue playing and putting time into this when they can just lose everything. I am sorry but i am furious, i just wanted to enjoy the game and stock my shelves and decorate my lil supermarket. I am not starting over and wasting away more time playing this god forsaken game, its not worth it.

Game has had so many bugs and annoying issues but now my color picker is broken when i try to purchase new shelves. After doing some research this is an issue they've known about since December and still haven't fixed. instead an independent Modder came up with a fix for it. Seriously greedy developers and despite the time I have in it, I have no problem never picking this game up again after seeing them try to release entire bundles of dlc that honestly should just come as part of the game and taking entirely too long to fix game breaking issues.

Game is fun at first, but the online orders and delivery system is bad. In a game about automation, I should not constantly get dinged that I need to pack an order and drive it somewhere, there is no way to turn this system off, it is very annoying and hurts the gameplay loop.

Trying to place a second cash register in multiplayer breaks the entire lobby. Tried different placements, different register types, all attempts broke the multiplayer lobby.

Fun little game, but over-rated. There are way better automation and market simulators available.

I bought the game because I thoroughly enjoyed the Game Grumps playthrough.  I'm 20 hours in and I love it.
2 things I would like to see to make it even better:
1.Automatic purchase scheduling.  Like Amazon, "if x item falls below y% in stock, auto purchase when cash is available."
2."Customer surveys":  or some way to see what customers are complaining about.  I'm happy to make adjustments but I can't always catch what they are saying.

Otherwise, highly addictive.  Keep up the great work!

Disappointing that rather than expand on the base game the dev decided to go the DLC route.
All the DLC's are items that should have been included in the base price.
$40+ for DLC's and the base game on a simulator game is a rediculous money grab. 

The game itself is okay, however replayability is low and late game becomes stagnent.

I don't like playing level after level with no rewards and I definitely don't like my level 78 game just disappearing back to the start.

It could be a fun game but it takes FOREVER to make money and level up. Plus when you finally do level up, most levels come with no rewards. You have to level up about 5 times to finally be able to add space to your store, get some new products to sell, get a larger storeroom. On top of all of that twice now when I tried to sign in to my game it was reset back to the start. The save file says Corrupt and I have to start all over again.  I saved my game and signed out. No issue. But now I can't play it. For these reasons, I do not recommend the game.
Fun game, could use more updates then just these dlcs like being able to make your store two stories/bigger and have multiple locations
Had a level 16 supermarket, I left the game, when I opened again and clicked my save it started me from tutorial. Hours of progress got lost just like that.
its very fun and if you like the feeling of progress then this is your game they also have many 3$ and 2$ DLCs and they are fun as well-fun to use.
An extremely good game, which has evolved quite a lot lately. Too bad the DLCs are weak.

I have enjoyed basically every aspect of the game. All of the mechanics like scanning, monitoring items, prices, other shops, and the items you can purchase online, and much much more content out there, I enjoy everything about it.

If you just want to play it for fun, it is very enjoyable. However, if you want to 100% the game, it does get VERY tedious. After about Day 200 or so, it goes into a loop of scanning products, waiting, repeat. I am on Day 685 now and I am still nowhere near obtaining 100% completion primarily due to how much the supermarket expansion costs near the end. I put in several hours of game time per day to get as much as I can get and even with an extremely optimal setup, it still takes such a long time.

Other than the 100% grind, the game is definitely a must-buy, must-try game for many people. It simulates most of 'being a store manager' and monitoring by making sure everything is flowing. It is definitely addictive at the beginning of the game as you build up your supermarket empire with a growing number of variety of both products and items. Although are constant minor bugs, it's still a brilliant game.

There is a bit of a learning curve on not getting ahead of yourself on costs.  Also, I'm no fan of the vehicle control style.  But, with patience you can do ok with it.  I haven't played too many sim games.  But this one is pretty relaxing, or you can spaz yourself out and grow the store quickly.
This game is the best and just vary fun i wish i cloud help build the game and i know how to use unity.

There's very little that I dislike about this game. It is FANTASTIC! I love it! I have always grown up loving checking people out at a cash register and this game can bring that to life for me. There is still little things that I don't really like but that doesn't matter, it's just my OCD kicking in. LOL! Anyway, this game is great, would recommend!
Started playing the free version, did not think I would get as hooked as I did to it. Great game especially if you tend to like organizing or micro managing everything (like myself!)
this game is super fun. it teaches people about money and how to take care of supermarkets
I Love this game but uh, I have recently encountered sentient boxes chasing me in multiplayer and other weird bugs. Otherwise, amazing game, really fun.

What can I honestly say? This game is rather addicting, especially as you gather more licenses. I truly enjoy the hecticness this can bring, but I really really despise the online orders section of the game. 
As someone who prefers restocking the store and focusing on that, running around the map to turn in an online order can be annoying. Oh well, I just let their time run out TBH. LOL. Either way, really fun game if you like to escape reality for awhile and play a sim.
Get's a little lag for some reason it gets worse as the day goes on. Fun game though

⊹ ࣪ ˖ **Recommended** ˖ ࣪ ⊹

Supermarket Simulator was basically the start of my simulator game era, hah. I love this game, and I honestly cannot wait to see more added to it. ⋆˚꩜｡

The gameplay is simple but so addicting. You stock shelves, order products, set prices, help customers, upgrade your store, and slowly build your little supermarket into something bigger. It has that satisfying “just one more day” feeling where you keep wanting to improve everything. ✧

I really enjoy how the game makes everyday store tasks feel fun. Organizing products, checking what needs to be restocked, watching customers come in, and trying to keep everything running smoothly is weirdly relaxing and stressful at the same time. ˚₊‧꒰ა ☆ ໒꒱ ‧₊˚

It is one of those simulator games that feels easy to get into, but still gives you plenty to work toward. I love seeing the store grow and slowly become more successful. ⟡

Overall, Supermarket Simulator is fun, chill, satisfying, and a great game if you enjoy management games, stocking shelves, and building something up from the ground. I had a lot of fun with it, and I am excited to see where the game goes next. ✨

Very fun game! Would reccomend. I also have some update ideas for you guys so you guys can add these features in-game.
1. Add delivery drivers to deliver online orders around the map
2. Add a buy all products x1 button
3. (Not required but nice to have) You can give out free food samples to customers
4. Add a membership card
5. Add an grocery bar where customers can relax at
6. Add tips

Hope these requests get added into the game! Keep going Nokta Games!
Addicting, even though I work as a cashier in my day to day life. 10/10

Most recent update fixed a lot of major lag issues I was getting, which was bad enough I stopped playing until it was solved. Now that it has been, it works as intended and I'll talk more about the game. It's a bit niche, but if you're someone who enjoys 'management' style day-to-day games, it hits a lot of that niche. It's very calming and good to play while listening to music or a novel or video. There's a pretty good mix of products, and the developers seem to be intending to add more (even if the DLC feels a bit pricey for a $20 game.)

The biggest issue at the high level is just that due to the mounting costs of store expansion it'll stall out LONG before you do all there is to do. There's a cap on how many customers you can get at the 'max' store level (in my experience about 180) and even by embracing your inner manger and hiring a boatload of people that limit won't be broached. Overall, if you're in the niche, I'd recommend it. I'm around 750 hours, most of that spent running while I'm editing or something, but still it's clearly got enough going on that I've racked up that much time.

update 1.4 broke co-op, I'll keep it downvoted until fixed. They decide to do QoL additions and break the ability to enjoy the game with anyone but yourself. great.

It's fun - I'll definitely recommend it for the first few hours of it.

But the issue is that if you want to profit, you want to purchase products cheap.
To buy them cheap you have to go to wholesale stores - which unlike instant delivery - require you to hand pick, buy and return it to the store. Which me and my gf were able to do well after grinding for a few days and getting a truck. But then I realized the game just becomes a restocking-fest. You can get workers to restock the store of course, but driving,  slowly buying everything, driving back, unloading and looping back.. it's gets pretty tedious - and the workers only go as far as the supermarket's entrance. It's fun to be at the supermarket itself, help costumers and catch thieves. But to really be able to push forward you're forced into this repeating scheme of buying everything constantly, leaving you mentally drained by the time you even save up for some upgrades and expansions for the store itself. It does feel like every item is just another label, it could be anything.. just need a proper shelf for it and set the price.

I'm not sure what I think about the game, as despite the start being really fun and building anticipation to progressing, I found out it's pretty repetitive and the same through most of your way up.
amazing game, I just wish the days were a little longer, my last save corrupted so now i'm restarting but its okay, I hope when i'm max level I can make my supermarket look like a real supermarket

Played this game wayyy too much. Can't play the game without mods, it's very badly optimized, restocking takes too long because the customers are not purchasing realistically and grabbing half a shelf of inventory each, so re-stockers cannot stock fast enough. I got bored eventually.
DLC is very cash grabby and decided to uninstall. It's a shame.

I like this game and want to play more. But seriously? Releasing (buggy) DLC all on the same day and charging around £6 for some DLC is damn right greedy and disgusting. You are not EA, sit down... Learn from indie developers like Paralives who doing all FREE  future updates for their game. Such a wrong move here.. It's very.... YIKES!
Nice game to have, i just hope that a small lorry will be add also to make it easy to load more stock rather than using pickup truck
I would enjoy this game, but for unknown reasons it keeps crashing on me every 10-15 minutes. I am addint my specs.
The game is very fun,i got it because i saw youutbers playing it and it made me want to download it now im almost maxed out on money and i have a very very big store!
I will be quitting school to make my store in real life and run walmart out of business, but not actually because then I would have no time to play this peak game.
good game if youre wanting something chill that doesnt require much brain power to unwind with
I can't own a supermarket in real life, but with this game I can live out my wildest supermarket owner dreams. 10/10
My first thoughts on the game were simply to build a store. Now, I want to maximize my profits while still being affordable and hit thieves with a bat.
Super fun game to just shut your brain off. Contemplating on buying the DLC too
very fun, easy and managable until you lose track of where everything is lol.

Not a game I would recomend, too many broken feautres and too much of a money grab. I was kinda enjoying it, till I stumbled upon Megastore Simulator, that cost me AUD$20, where Supermarket cost me around AUD$70 for the game and all the DLC.  All the stuff in the DLC for supermarket is included in Megastore, from the start.  So no, would not recomend this game at all.  If you want a store simulator, look elsewhere.

Have had this game for years and it has always been very buggy and slap-dashed.  The gameplay loop was extremely addictive and enjoyable for a bit but the scaling never made a lot of sense without needing cheats to get anywhere near the amount of upgrades required to access all of the late game stuff. 

The half-cost supply retailers around town provide an option to increase profits but the amount of customers in store necessitates a staff size that eats up so much overhead that I found myself doing days and days and days for a net gain of like 500/day without the entire game loop being about driving to and from those supply retailers the whole game, and that's without trying to keep up with delivery orders (which do have a decent profit margin)

This is before trying to get back into the game today which apparently isn't possible anymore since the game won't start on load, even after a restart, and looking at how the dev decided to copy paste a ton of low effort DLCs as updates in a flash attempt at getting more money, instead of addressing the hundreds of bugs still present has me ready to leave this one in the dust.

Other games like Megastore Simulator, which takes this game loop and seems to do a way better job of recreating the supermarket aesthetic where this game has always been better called Corner Store Simulator, tells me the crown for this subgenre has been passed on.  
And as much as this whole simulator subgenre is RIDDLED with AI slop, AI-esthetic, smothered in typos, DESPERATELY in need of a team of testers, copy-pasted sheen -- at least other titles seem to be doing a much better job of delivering a less cash-grabby and clunky experience.

my mom loves this game and i like playing with her the game is fantastic and realistic with there stealing and complaints we work retail so we would know any way would recommend for those that like managing game a bit of a slow burn but when you start to get going its gets going but there are some problems in multiplayer one is when i join my mom and catch a thief i cant pick any of the items up lucky she can though i crashed her game by throwing boxes onto the road to block cars from moving than lined up the car parallel to the storage next door and throwing boxes at the car in just the right way to move it over. over time get it down the stares than it starts clipping throw the wall a lot of silly things can happen at this point i seen a car get thrown violently but if you can get about six in there congratulations you crashed the game 10/10 we loving it

very good simulator game
graphics 6.5/10 there pretty good but nothing extreme
proformance 7.5/10 most people could run it but its not extremely smooth on a lower end system
story 0/0 there's no story 
gameplay 8/10 its a simulator can get boring after many hours
overall 7.8 pretty good game overall
very addicting, so much fun, realistic
very satisfying game, pretty awesome. played this for like 4 hours straight, and never got bored.

The game is really good, but you should add more licenses for new products, because that’s what keeps players motivated and excited to continue playing.

You should also make it easier for content creators to work with the game, since with every update, custom contents stop working. That becomes frustrating for people who invest their time into making the game richer and more enjoyable for everyone.

Looking forward to hearing your feedback and seeing future updates.

Loving this simulator game.  Lots of product and store upgrades available for spening your hard earned money on.  You're also not stuack to your shop and can explore the area for other product stockists and opportunities.
game good sometimes boring repetiive but i havent played since caseoh played it so idk they might have added some new stuff do its not boring and repetitve.
Be very Careful with Loans. Other then that its amazing. PS. The loans dont make the game bad, its how you use them that could lead to ruining your whole business.
I have neurological disorders and this is a fun game to pass the time while stuck at home.

Another perfect example of a studio that had success releasing a game in alpha, then after release got greedy and released updates as DLC's to make more  money. This game IS NOT worth 40+ bucks for the game and the dlc's which are basically new products and shelves., Game was a lot of fun when it first got finished, now though there are definetly alternatives that are much much cheaper and just as good.
This game is perfect for obsessive and control-oriented brains. Very relaxing when I need something to focus on. Easy to get lost in. I love organizing!
amazing game, one of the best offline games, ive ever played. Great for relaxing and spending some of your free time on it.
Está bien, no creo que me pille los dlcs pero está bien, sin más, típico simulador
This game is fun and you can play it over and over with cool things to buy and other fun things
Great game, appropriate difficulty and progression. Enjoyable
Really wanted to play because of jacksepticeye, besides that, I really enjoy this game and it's so much fun!

Great game, very chill, very nice to play.

Noticed one bug when you fire a security guard who is carrying a box outside the store with an item in it. The item will stay floating in the air where he was carrying it indefinitely.

Supermarket Simulator is a surprisingly addicting business simulation game where you run your own grocery store from the ground up. The gameplay is simple but satisfying: you buy products, stock shelves, set prices, help customers, scan items at checkout, and slowly expand your store as you make more money. It gives a nice feeling of progression because every upgrade makes your supermarket feel more successful.

One of the best parts of Supermarket Simulator is how hands-on it is. You are not just managing numbers from a menu; you actually walk around the store, organize products, restock shelves, handle the register, and make sure customers are satisfied. Choosing what to sell and adjusting prices adds a business strategy side, while the physical work of stocking and checking out customers keeps the game active.

However, the game can become repetitive after a while. A lot of the gameplay is based on buying inventory, placing products, checking customers out, and repeating the same routine each day. Some parts can also feel slow, especially early in the game when you do not have many upgrades or employees. Still, the simple loop is also what makes it relaxing and easy to keep playing.

Overall, Supermarket Simulator is a fun and satisfying game for people who enjoy business management, store-building, and relaxing simulation games. It may not be the most complex simulator, but it is easy to get into and very enjoyable once your store starts growing.

Rating: 8/10

This game has come a long way from when it came out in early access.  The game is much more polished now, and the developer keeps supporting the game diligently.  I think that this is the best simulator game that I have played.
My lovely Grandchild loves to play this game when he comes to my house on the weekend. I definitely recomend for anybody who enjoys simulation games!!
I love the simplicity of the game mechanics and how easy it is to get stuck playing for hours.
its a little laggy at some points, but overall a great game!
Idk how but this game makes a mundane and tedious job fun and endearing. 10/10 would recommend.
This game is so nostalgic and fun recommended if you want a chill game

good game but the stocker employee is a little bug . I'm not sure why he keeps saying storage is full when its empty and than he stops restocking. its so annoying. but other than that good game i enjoy it so far.
Such a fun game for those who thrive on ocd and organization! I love creating a little store I get to run!
Fun and cute game, some bugs when playing multiplayer with friends. The customers always show up at closing time, the whole ♥♥♥♥♥♥♥ town decides that's the best time to do their daily shopping lol
very perfect game, can get addictive but in a good way.

I love this game develop through out the years. They have put in effort in making this game good. There are still upsetting bugs like achievement bugs where you do not unlock them despite doing what they say. Overall excellent game but tiring because all the stock you buy, you have to put in storage so it take 20 min to pack everthing into a storage room if you are far in the game.

If you're not good at PVP and like More calm games this is the game for you. it Almost feels retro with the style, also It's very good because you don't progress very Fast I think this is a good game and I would  recommend to other people
I've loved this game since pre-release and I love it to this day. Thank you creators for consistency!!!
its really addicting and fun

at the point in the game that I am in the game requires tens of thousands to upgrade stuff and my daily profit keeps going DOWN not up. despite there being more and more customers. I have to have a calculator in order to make the small amount of money that I am. I shouldn't have to have my prices set to 1.05 of the recommended market price.... (which is why the calculator) its a cute game until you get higher level.
Using ai slop for their cover picture was enough to make me stop playing. smh

Alright first of all I tolerate this game it's fun and enjoyable to a point. I'm not gonna sit here and say that it's downright unplayable cause it ain't but here's the thing.... I just realized the other day that there is DLC content to add more products to the shelves. I'm only saying this once Nokta Games. Explain to me why I have to pay more money for stuff that should be in the base game for free. Because I ain't gonna lie nobody is happy about it if you want people to play your game this ain't the way to do it. Also keep in mind that I stopped playing this for months to wait for more content and when I came back nothing changed except the tablet and crate feature. Don't even get me started with not having enough room to add more shelves in storage area and the actual store area it's honestly stupid how long it takes just to get more room which is probably like 4x4 every time. This game was alright at the beginning back then I understood because it just came out. But now I feel like I'm playing a pay to win game. I'd rather go play Retail Tycoon 2 on Roblox.
It was fun while it lasted, and mods made it great, but it's been DLC'd to death.
the original shop simulator. co-op and mods a huge plus. devs fixed a game breaking bug in under 24 hours. 9.9/10
while the game it's self is ok i refused to pay out more money for a DLC's that are clearly bugged.
THIS GAME IS AWESOME! i recommend it for people who like to play games that allow u to run a fake store/compony

And maybe the just 6 restockers is why I thinking on stop plat Supermarked Simulator?
the workers be looking dead while working, 10/10 supermarket experience
Fun! Challenge yourself to see if you got what it takes to build a 5 star supermarket!

Glitches galore! The multiplayer has broken our save file. Money is gone, store name back to default, etc. That's if I can even get it to load in. Sometimes it freezes immediately and crashes. Also no one is able to join my broken multiplayer save. I don't even think its creating a server since the "session time" stays at 0. I think they broke something after the recent update which sounds plausible if you read the negative reviews and search on Reddit. Very frustrating to have something you enjoyed become unplayable after a simple update. Save your money on this one.
Really relaxing and enjoyable having your own supermarket and managing it.

It took a bit for me to really get into it, but I'm honestly hooked. There are a few aspects I still haven't figured out, and a FAQ would be nice, but I'm sure I'll get it as I continue. I'm very glad I got it.
